narrative: Your loan application was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which looks at patterns from past borrowers in 1970s Germany to assess risk, and it determined there's an 80% chance you'd face challenges repaying, leading to the denial. This high probability means the model sees your situation as similar to those who struggled with loans in the past, despite your stable job as a skilled worker, long employment history of over seven years, and owning your housing. The strongest factor pushing toward this bad credit outlook was your checking account status showing a negative balance, which stands out because only about 17% of approved applicants had no funds there, making it a major red flag for repayment ability. Next in influence was the 60-month duration you requested for this business loan, far longer than the typical 20 months for those approved, as extended terms often signal higher risk in historical data. Your savings under 100 DM also contributed notably toward the denial, since over half of successful applicants had at least that much set aside, highlighting limited financial cushions. These top three elements together outweighed positives like having no other installment plans, no additional debtors, and just two existing credits at our bank. Compared to approved applicants, your requested amount of 6,836 DM is more than double their average, and your past payment delays further amplified concerns despite your registered telephone and one dependent. While factors like being a foreign worker aligned with most approved cases, they couldn't offset the key risks. We understand this is disappointing, especially with your established residence of four years and property situation. To improve future chances, consider building up your checking and savings balances, shortening the loan term, and strengthening your payment history—perhaps by taking smaller loans first or providing more details on your business purpose. Feel free to reapply after addressing these, or contact us to discuss options tailored to your steady employment and housing stability.
